info.title: KeyGo Server API
info.description:
Enterprise authentication service — open source alternative for centralized identity, user and access management.

⚠️ Bootstrap security is disabled — all endpoints are public.

tags.name: OIDC Discovery
tags.description: Public OIDC discovery and JWKS endpoints — no authentication required. Returns raw JSON (not BaseResponse) for compatibility with third-party OAuth2/OIDC libraries.

tags.name: Client Apps
tags.description: OAuth2 client application management per tenant — requires Bearer JWT

tags.name: OAuth2 / Authorization
tags.description: OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE flow, token exchange, refresh token rotation and client_credentials grant — all public endpoints (no X-KEYGO-ADMIN)

tags.name: Registration
tags.description: Public user self-registration and email verification endpoints

tags.name: Platform
tags.description: Public platform endpoints — no authentication required

tags.name: Tenants
tags.description: Tenant lifecycle management — requires Bearer JWT

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Administrative endpoints — requires ADMIN Bearer JWT

tags.name: Memberships
tags.description: User access to applications — requires Bearer JWT

tags.name: Account Profile
tags.description: Self-service profile management — requires Bearer token (no X-KEYGO-ADMIN)

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User identity management per tenant — requires Bearer JWT

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific user by its UUID within the tenant.

put.description: Updates the firstName and lastName of an existing user.

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific OAuth2 client application by its `clientId`.

404.description: Client app or tenant not found

put.description: Updates name, description, redirect URIs, grants or scopes of an existing client app.

404.description: Client app or tenant not found

put.description: Suspends an active tenant. A suspended tenant cannot be used for authentication.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique slug identifier of the tenant

put.description: Reactivates a suspended or pending tenant, allowing it to process authentication requests again.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique slug identifier of the tenant

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all tenants. Supports optional filtering by status and partial name match. Requires ADMIN role.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by tenant status (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, PENDING)

post.description: Creates a new tenant with the given name and owner email. A URL-friendly slug is automatically generated from the name.

get.description: Returns all user accounts registered under the specified tenant.

post.description: Creates a new user account scoped to the specified tenant. Username and email must be unique within the tenant.

409.description: Email or username already exists in this tenant

post.description: Administratively resets the password of a user within the tenant.
Validates a user's credentials (email or username + password) within the tenant. Returns user data on success.

401.description: Invalid credentials or admin key missing

post.description:
Multi-grant token endpoint. The `grant_type` field selects the behaviour:

| `grant_type` | Required fields | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| `authorization_code` | `client_id`, `code`, `redirect_uri`, `code_verifier` | `access_token` + `id_token` + `refresh_token` |
| `refresh_token` | `client_id`, `refresh_token` | new `access_token` + `id_token` + rotated `refresh_token` |
| `client_credentials` | `client_id`, `client_secret`, `scope` | `access_token` only (M2M) |

400.description: Invalid request — missing required fields for the selected grant_type

404.description: Tenant or client app not found

post.description: Revokes a refresh token per RFC 7009. The endpoint is **idempotent** — if the token does not exist or has already been revoked, 200 OK is returned anyway to prevent token existence enumeration. No authentication header required.

200.description: Token revoked (or silently ignored if unknown)

get.description: List all memberships for a user or app (query params determine filter)

post.description: Grant user access to an application with specified roles

404.description: User, app, or tenant not found

get.description: Returns all OAuth2 client applications registered under the specified tenant.

post.description: Registers a new OAuth2 client application under the specified tenant. Returns the `clientId` and a one-time visible `clientSecret` — store it securely.
Validates the 6-digit code received by email. If valid, the user is activated (status → ACTIVE). If expired, a new code must be requested.

200.description: Email verified — user is now active

422.description: Verification code has expired — request a new one

post.description: Generates a new `clientSecret` for a confidential client app, invalidating the previous one. The new secret is returned once — store it securely.

404.description: Client app or tenant not found

post.description: Generates and sends a new verification code. Only allowed once the previous code has expired (30-minute window).

409.description: Current code is still active — wait until it expires

post.description: Creates a new user with PENDING status and sends a 6-digit verification code to the provided email. The code is valid for 30 minutes.

400.description: Invalid request body or duplicate email/username

404.description: Tenant or client app not found

get.description: List all roles defined for a client application

post.description: Create a new role within a client application

400.description: Invalid input or duplicate role code

post.summary: Submit credentials and issue authorization code
post.description:
Authenticates the user within the tenant and issues a one-time authorization code. Requires an active authorization session established by `GET /oauth2/authorize`.

The returned `code` must be exchanged for tokens via `POST /oauth2/token` (`grant_type=authorization_code`) using the original PKCE `code_verifier`.

400.description: No authorization state in session — call GET /oauth2/authorize first

get.description: Returns the complete profile of the authenticated user (all OIDC extended fields). Requires Authorization: Bearer <access_token>.

patch.description: Partially updates the profile of the authenticated user. Only non-null fields are updated (PATCH semantics). Requires Authorization: Bearer <access_token>.

get.description: Returns identity claims of the authenticated user per OIDC Core 1.0 §5.3. Requires `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` — the token must have been issued by the `POST /oauth2/token` endpoint with `openid` scope.

Starts the OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE flow. Validates that the tenant is active, the client app exists and the redirect URI is registered. Stores the authorization state in the HTTP session so that `POST /account/login` can retrieve it.

**Flow:** `GET /authorize` → `POST /account/login` → `POST /oauth2/token`

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Optional opaque value to maintain state

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge (BASE64URL of SHA-256 of verifier)

200.description: Authorization initiated — client app metadata returned

404.description: Tenant or client app not found

get.description: Returns the OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 document for the specified tenant. This document contains all endpoints (authorization, token, userinfo, jwks_uri) and supported capabilities required for automatic client configuration.

200.description: OIDC discovery document — raw JSON per OIDC Discovery 1.0 spec

get.description: Returns the JSON Web Key Set (RFC 7517) containing the active and retired RSA public keys used to sign JWT tokens for the specified tenant. Used by relying parties to verify token signatures.
Retrieves tenant details by its unique slug identifier.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique slug identifier of the tenant

get.description: Returns public metadata about the running service: title, name, and version. This endpoint does not require authentication.
Returns the full catalog of KeyGo response codes, separated into success codes and failure codes. This endpoint does not require authentication.
Returns aggregated counts of tenants (by status), users (by status), client apps and active signing keys. Requires ADMIN role.
Returns a full aggregated view of the platform: service status, security metrics, tenant/user/app/membership counts, rankings, pending actions and recent activity. Requires ADMIN role.

delete.description: Remove user access to an application

BearerAuth.description: Bearer JWT token issued by KeyGo OAuth2/OIDC endpoints.
